```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  p <- sample(setdiff(seq(0.10,0.90,0.05),0.5),1)
  valor <- sample(c(0,1),1)
  ans <- if(valor==1) p else 1-p
  vals <- round(c(ans,1-ans,p*(1-p),p^2,(1-p)^2),3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Para $X=\mathbf{1}_A$ com $P(A)=`r p`$, qual é $p_X(`r valor`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A indicadora tem distribuição Bernoulli:
$P(X=1)=p$ e $P(X=0)=1-p$. Assim,
$p_X(`r valor`)=`r sprintf("%.3f",ans)`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S6Q10_indicadora_Bernoulli
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
